entries.definition:
m–r–ʾ (of food) to be wholesome and palatable, to be digestible,
food passage; a human being, man, woman, wife, one person;
‘chivalrous’ character, generosity of nature, to be humane,
humanity. Of this root, five forms occur 38 times in the Qur’an:
مَرِيئًا marīʾan once; مَرْء marʾ four times; امْرُؤ ʾimruʾ seven times;
امْرَأَة ʾimraʾatun 24 times and امْرَأَتَان ʾimraʾatān twice.

مَرِيئًا marīʾan [quasi-act. part. used adverbially] palatable,
nourishing, wholesome *(4:4) فَكُلُوهُ هَنِيئًا مَرِيئًا you may assimilate it
[in your own property] by all means [lit. with pleasure and
health-giving appetite].

مَرْء marʾ [n.] a person, one (8:24) وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَحُولُ بَيْنَ الْمَرْءِ وَقَلْبِهِ
now that God stands between a person and his heart (or, his
secret thoughts).

امْرُؤ ʾimruʾ [n. in the nom.; acc. امْرَأ ʾimraʾ; gen. امْرِئ ʾimriʾ]
person, one (70:38) أَيَطْمَعُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُدْخَلَ جَنَّةَ نَعِيمٍ does every one
of them hope to enter a Garden of bliss?

امْرَأَة ʾimraʾatun [n.; dual امْرَأَتَان ʾimraʾatān] 1 woman
(27:23) إِنِّي وَجَدْتُ امْرَأَةً تَمْلِكُهُمْ I found a woman ruling them 2 wife
(19:8) وَكَانَتِ امْرَأَتِي عَاقِرًا when my wife is barren.
